visionary.name: Neha Jivan, OT (Hons)
visionary.imageAlt: A warm professional portrait of Neha Jivan, paediatric occupational therapist.
visionary.bio: Neha Jivan is a resourceful Occupational Therapist with a Bachelor of Occupational Therapy Honours degree from UKZN. Her community service year at Cato Manor CHC opened the door to working with children from many different backgrounds, and it is there that her love for paediatric therapy deepened. She thrives on connecting with diverse people, cultures, and personalities, bringing strong communication, collaboration, and thoughtful organisation into every family's journey. Neha's work is grounded in curiosity and care: getting down to a child's level, meeting them through play, mapping out practical solutions when challenges arise, and using gentle trial and error to find what works best. She celebrates the small milestones because those brave little steps grow into confidence, independence, and lasting change. Currently completing the DIR/Floortime introductory course, she remains committed to life-long learning, critical thinking, and holistic care delivered with compassion, warmth, and steady motivation.
visionary.quote: Great things are done by a series of small things brought together. In therapy, every tiny win matters.

steps.title: Listen First
steps.description: We begin with a relaxed conversation about your child, your routines, your concerns, and the moments you would love to feel easier.

steps.title: Assess Through Play
steps.description: A gentle OT assessment helps us understand sensory needs, fine motor skills, coordination, confidence, and everyday independence.

steps.title: Build Tiny Wins
steps.description: Sessions turn therapy goals into playful activities that build skills step by step, without losing the warmth and fun children need.

steps.title: Carry It Home
steps.description: You leave with practical strategies for home and school, with regular check-ins to celebrate progress and adjust the plan.

roles.title: Paediatric Occupational Therapist
roles.summary: Neha currently works within a dedicated paediatric occupational therapy practice, supporting children and families through assessment, treatment planning, parent collaboration, and playful therapeutic intervention.

roles.title: Community Service Occupational Therapist
roles.organisation: Cato Manor CHC, Department of Health South Africa
roles.summary: Her community service year offered rich exposure to children and families from diverse backgrounds, strengthening her clinical adaptability, community outreach experience, and commitment to holistic care.

education.qualification: Bachelor of Occupational Therapy (Honours), Public Health
education.summary: A professional foundation in client-centred care, health promotion, clinical reasoning, and purposeful occupation.
Distinctions in Dramatic Arts and Afrikaans, with involvement in Eco-club, Social Badminton, and the Hindu Student Association.
